methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $78M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $500M.

data.narrative:
WeaveGrid is a Series B climate and energy company founded by Apoorv Bhargava and John Taggart that has raised $78M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 5 private peers at the same stage and sector, supplemented by 1 public comparable (LNZA), creating an 8-company comp set dominated by private transactions.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $78M total raised as a proxy for current value, applying a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B to subsequent-stage step-ups, yielding $148M. Peer-median analysis references 5 same-stage, same-sector peers—Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), and Cemvita ($120M)—producing a median valuation of $310M with a range of $120M to $500M.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $126M (low) to $356M (high), with a median of $229M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty in comparable applicability: peer valuations span a 4.2x range, and WeaveGrid's specific technology differentiation, revenue traction, and capital efficiency relative to this cohort remain key drivers of where within the range a fair value would settle. The stage-progression method yields the lower bound, while peer comparables anchor the upper end.
